title: Fix Admin Data Entry (Students, Fees, Courses), Nigerian Currency Formatting, and Role-Based Service Menu

requirements.text: Fix the Admin Panel so that adding students, fees, and courses fully persists end-to-end. In the Motoko backend, ensure addFeeItem, addCourse, and any student-creation/profile-update functions correctly write to stable state and that the corresponding getters return all stored records reliably. In the frontend, wire the FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, and StudentManagementPage forms to their respective backend mutation hooks so that submitted data is saved to the backend (not just local state) and the lists refresh immediately after submission without a page reload.

quotes: I was still unable to add fees enable me to add students, fees,courses ,and every other

acceptanceCriteria: Admin can add a fee item (name, amount, programme, session) and it appears in the fee list immediately after submission.
Admin can add a course (code, name, semester, programme) and it immediately appears in course lists across ProgrammeManagementPage, CourseRegistrationPage, and ResultsManagementPage.
Admin can create/register a new student profile from the admin panel and the student appears in the student list immediately.
All added data persists across page reloads (confirmed by refreshing and checking the lists).
Form fields are cleared and a success toast/message is shown after each successful submission.
Error messages are shown if the backend call fails.

requirements.text: Add Nigerian Naira (₦) currency formatting with thousand separators to all fee-related amount fields and displays throughout the application. This includes: the FeeManagementPage fee list and input field, the FeeStatementPage outstanding and paid fee amounts, the PaymentHistoryPage payment amounts, the ReceiptPage payment total, and any other page displaying monetary values. Format numbers as ₦1,000,000.00 using the Nigerian locale (en-NG) or equivalent Intl.NumberFormat formatting.

quotes: On the fees add a Nigerian currency separator for thousand

acceptanceCriteria: All fee amounts display with the ₦ symbol and comma thousand separators (e.g., ₦150,000.00).
The fee amount input field in FeeManagementPage shows a ₦ prefix and formats the displayed value.
FeeStatementPage outstanding and paid totals are formatted as Nigerian Naira.
ReceiptPage total amount is formatted as Nigerian Naira.
Formatting is consistent across all fee-related pages.

requirements.text: After login, the navigation sidebar/menu for each role (Student, Staff, Parent, Admin) must display a contextual list of all services and features available to that role. For Students: Course Registration, Fee Statement, Results, Transcript, Hostel Application, Payment History, Announcements. For Staff: My Profile, Assigned Courses, Enrolled Students, Announcements, Exams & Records Portal. For Parents: Linked Student Overview, Student Results, Fee Status, Course Registrations. For Admins: Manage Students, Manage Staff, Manage Courses/Programmes, Manage Fees, Admissions, Results Management, Hostel Applications, Messaging/Announcements, Exams & Records Portal. These service links should be visible in the dashboard sidebar or the role-based dropdown menu in the Navigation component after a user is authenticated.

quotes: on the student profile after login I want the menu to also have the list of services that the students is served with applicable to staff login and other logins

acceptanceCriteria: A logged-in student sees all student-applicable service links in their dashboard sidebar or navigation menu.
A logged-in staff member sees all staff-applicable service links in their dashboard sidebar or navigation menu.
A logged-in parent sees all parent-applicable service links in their dashboard sidebar or navigation menu.
A logged-in admin sees all admin-applicable service links in their dashboard sidebar or navigation menu.
Clicking each service link navigates to the correct page.
The service menu is not visible to unauthenticated users.
Each role only sees services relevant to their role (no cross-role leakage).

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo).
Use Intl.NumberFormat with en-NG locale or equivalent for Nigerian Naira formatting — do not use a third-party currency library.
Generate migration.mo only if stable backend state variables are added or changed.
nonGoals: Redesigning the overall visual theme or layout of existing pages.
Adding new payment methods beyond existing Stripe integration.
Building a new separate frontend application for this request.
Changing the Internet Identity authentication flow.
